metadata.name: ww-timeline
metadata.description: A timeline element for displaying chronological events, milestones, process steps, or history entries with repeated custom event content.
features: Left, right, alternate, top, or bottom event alignment depending on layout
Custom event card content through the timelineElement slot
Click events on event content and markers
Repeated slot context through context.item.data and context.item.index

properties.name: markerIconOnOff
properties.description: Whether each marker should display a system icon.

properties.name: markerIcon
properties.description: System icon name displayed inside markers when markerIconOnOff is true.

properties.name: markerIconColor
properties.description: Color of the marker icon when markerIconOnOff is true.

properties.name: markerIconSize
properties.description: CSS size of the marker icon when markerIconOnOff is true.

properties.name: markerSize
properties.description: CSS width and height of each timeline marker.

properties.name: markerBackgroundColor
properties.description: Background color of each timeline marker.

properties.name: connectorColor
properties.description: Color of the connector line between timeline events.

properties.name: connectorWidth
properties.description: CSS width of the connector line.

properties.name: eventsAlignment
properties.description: Event alignment. For vertical timelines use left, right, or alternate. For horizontal timelines use top or bottom.

properties.name: data
properties.description: Array of event objects rendered by the timeline. Each object is exposed to the timelineElement slot as context.item.data.

events.name: timeline:click
events.description: Triggered when the user clicks an event content area.

value.title: Project kickoff
value.description: Initial milestone data object from the timeline data array.

events.name: timeline:markerClick
events.description: Triggered when the user clicks an event marker.

value.title: Project kickoff
value.description: Initial milestone data object from the timeline data array.

localContext.description: The current event object from the data array, available inside the timelineElement slot.
Zero-based index of the current repeated timeline event, available inside the timelineElement slot.

slots.name: timelineElement
slots.description: Required repeated content element rendered once for each item in data. Use a ww-div card with children bound to context.item.data fields.

specifications: This element renders one timeline event for each object in the data array.
Always provide a non-empty data array for static examples; an empty data array renders no event cards.
Always provide a timelineElement slot child. Without it, the timeline markers can render with empty event content.
Use ww-div as the timelineElement slot child and add ww-text or ww-icon children inside that card.
Inside timelineElement children, bind text and styles to context.item.data and context.item.index. Common data fields are date, title, description, status, category, and icon.
For vertical timelines, eventsAlignment must be left, right, or alternate. Prefer alternate for showcase or milestone pages.
For horizontal timelines, eventsAlignment must be top or bottom. Use horizontal layout only when the parent has enough width or horizontal overflow.
Set markerIconOnOff to true only when markerIcon is also provided and the icon name exists in the project's installed icon sets.

Do not invent markerIcon values. Use only icon names that are known to exist in the current project. If no installed icon-set information is available, set markerIconOnOff to false and omit markerIcon.
When the phosphor-fill icon set is available, safe timeline marker examples include phosphor-fill/calendar-fill, phosphor-fill/check-fill, phosphor-fill/flag-fill, and phosphor-fill/seal-check-fill.
The timeline:click and timeline:markerClick events both return the clicked item as event.value.
Do not create separate sibling cards outside the timeline for each data item; event cards belong in the repeated timelineElement slot.

examples.description: Vertical alternate project timeline with repeated event cards.
examples.example: {"uid":"project_timeline","tag":"ww-timeline","name":"Project Timeline","content":{"default":{"data":[{"date":"Jan 2026","title":"Discovery","description":"Define goals and collect requirements.","status":"done"},{"date":"Feb 2026","title":"Prototype","description":"Build the first interactive experience.","status":"active"},{"date":"Mar 2026","title":"Launch","description":"Release the final project.","status":"upcoming"}],"timelineLayout":"vertical","eventsAlignment":"alternate","markerShape":"circle","markerSize":"28px","markerBackgroundColor":"#2563EB","connectorColor":"#BFDBFE","connectorWidth":"3px","markerIconOnOff":true,"markerIcon":"phosphor-fill/calendar-fill","markerIconColor":"#FFFFFF","markerIconSize":"14px"}},"style":{"default":{"width":"100%","display":"block"}},"slots":[{"slotKey":"timelineElement","elements":["timeline_card"]}]}{"uid":"timeline_card","tag":"ww-div","name":"Timeline Card","style":{"default":{"display":"flex","flexDirection":"column","gap":"8px","padding":"20px","backgroundColor":"#FFFFFF","border":"1px solid #E5E7EB","borderRadius":"16px","boxShadow":"0 8px 24px rgba(15,23,42,0.08)"}},"slots":[{"slotKey":"children","elements":["timeline_date","timeline_title","timeline_description"]}]}{"uid":"timeline_date","tag":"ww-text","name":"Timeline Date","content":{"default":{"tag":"p","text":{"en":{"__wwtype":"f","code":"context.item.data.date"}}}},"style":{"default":{"fontSize":"14px","fontWeight":"600","color":"#2563EB"}}}{"uid":"timeline_title","tag":"ww-text","name":"Timeline Title","content":{"default":{"tag":"h3","text":{"en":{"__wwtype":"f","code":"context.item.data.title"}}}},"style":{"default":{"fontSize":"22px","fontWeight":"700","color":"#111827"}}}{"uid":"timeline_description","tag":"ww-text","name":"Timeline Description","content":{"default":{"tag":"p","text":{"en":{"__wwtype":"f","code":"context.item.data.description"}}}},"style":{"default":{"fontSize":"15px","lineHeight":"1.6","color":"#4B5563"}}}

examples.description: Horizontal process timeline with compact cards.
